description_long: This is a traditional hand drum, likely a djembe, featuring a goblet-shaped wooden body with a natural skin drumhead tightly stretched across the top. The drumhead is secured with a network of white and orange rope lacing, forming a diamond pattern around the upper section. The wood body is smoothly carved, with a slightly flared base and a more pronounced, rounded upper section. The drumhead appears to be made from animal hide, giving it a slightly textured, matte finish. The ropes are woven through dark, circular anchor points just below the drumhead, providing both structural integrity and decorative contrast. The overall appearance suggests a robust, hand-crafted instrument suitable for outdoor performances or street music in urban environments.
description: A hand-crafted wooden djembe drum with a natural hide drumhead, secured by white and orange rope lacing in a diamond pattern. The drum features a smooth, goblet-shaped body and decorative rope work.
description_view_0: Front view showing the drumhead, diamond-patterned rope lacing, and the smooth, slightly flared wooden base.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the rope lacing and the transition between the upper and lower sections of the wooden body.
description_view_2: Another side view with clear visibility of the rope pattern and the rounded drumhead edge.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the continuous rope lacing and the unadorned, smooth wooden base.
